perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will not use, manufacture or sell any document, schematic, drawing, photograph, specification, specimen or any other material making up any part of the Confidential Information, or use any Co nfidential Information as a basis for the design or creation of any items or ot her means without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party may use Confidential Information to evaluate potential business opportunities. The receiving party will not use, manufacture or sell any document, schematic, drawing, photograph, specification, specimen or any other material making up any part of the Confidential Information, or use any Co nfidential Information as a basis for the design or creation of any items or ot her means without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence allowing the use of Confidential Information to evaluate business opportunities directly contradicts the immediately following sentence, which states that the receiving party cannot use Confidential Information for any purpose without prior written consent. This creates ambiguity about whether and when use is permissible, especially when the 'evaluation' phase transitions to 'design' or 'creation'. The contradiction introduces uncertainty on how to enforce appropriate restriction on use of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party may use Confidential Information to evaluate potential business opportunities. The receiving party will not use, manufacture or sell any document, schematic, drawing, photograph, specification, specimen or any other material making up any part of the Confidential Information, or use any Co nfidential Information as a basis for the design or creation of any items or ot her means without the prior written consent of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The added sentence grants permission to use Confidential Information for evaluating business opportunities. The subsequent sentence prohibits any use of Confidential Information without prior written consent. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict within the same section, making it uncertain under what circumstances the use of Confidential Information is permitted, therefore making the answer 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: For a period of five (5) years from the date of receiving it, all Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party, will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons empl oyed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set fo rth above, and will be protected with the same degree of care as the recei ving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party indefinitely, and will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons employed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set forth above, and will be protected with the same degree of care as the receiving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care. However, after five (5) years from the date of receiving it, the receiving party may disclose Confidential Information provided that it informs the disclosing party 30 days in advance.
perturbation.explanation: The first sentence says confidentiality is indefinite, contradicting the second which creates an exception allowing disclosure after five years with 30 days' notice. This creates confusion over the true duration of confidentiality. Is it indefinite, or five years with notice? The introduction of the 30-day advance notice complicates enforcement, as it's unclear what recourse the disclosing party has during those 30 days. This change directly contradicts the stated purpose of indefinitely maintaining confidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party indefinitely, and will not be disclosed to any third party or to any persons employed in its business other than those having a need to know for the purposes set forth above, and will be protected with the same degree of care as the receiving party normally uses in the protection of its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no case with any less degree than reasonable care. However, after five (5) years from the date of receiving it, the receiving party may disclose Confidential Information provided that it informs the disclosing party 30 days in advance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Confidential Information will be maintained in confidence by the receiving party indefinitely', which directly contradicts the subsequent statement 'However, after five (5) years from the date of receiving it, the receiving party may disclose Confidential Information provided that it informs the disclosing party 30 days in advance.' This introduces a clear contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality, making it uncertain whether it is indefinite or limited to five years with a 30-day notice period. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, creating an in-text contradiction as stated in the explanation. The Structural Flaws definition states that in-text contradictions arise when 'a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.' Here, the conflicting implications pertain to whether confidentiality is indefinite or has an exception, as this causes uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.

perturbation.original_text: The restrictions above will not apply to Confide ntial Information which: (a) Is known by the receiving party at the time of receipt; (b) Is or becomes a part of the public domain witho ut a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (c) The receiving party obtains from a third party under conditions permitting its disclosure to others; (d) Is independently developed by the receiving par ty; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to judicial action or gov ernment regulations, provided that the receiving party notifies the furn ishing party prior to such disclosure and co-operates with the furnishing part y in the event the furnishing party elects to legally contest and avoi d such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The restrictions above will not apply to Confidential Information which: (a) Is known by the receiving party at the time of receipt; (b) Is or becomes a part of the public domain without a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (c) The receiving party obtains from a third party under conditions permitting its disclosure to others; (d) Is independently developed by the receiving party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to judicial action or government regulations, provided that the receiving party notifies the furnishing party prior to such disclosure and co-operates with the furnishing party in the event the furnishing party elects to legally contest and avoid such disclosure. Notwithstanding the above, information originally disclosed under this agreement but later falling under (b), (c), or (d) shall remain confidential for one year from the date it met the new criteria.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence creates a contradiction by stating that even if information becomes public, obtained from a third party, or independently developed, it *still* remains confidential for one year if it was originally disclosed under the agreement. This directly contradicts the exceptions listed in (b), (c), and (d). The added constraint significantly undermines the exceptions outlined in (b), (c), and (d). This change introduces a conflict of interpretation as to what information is truly exempt from the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The restrictions above will not apply to Confidential Information which: (a) Is known by the receiving party at the time of receipt; (b) Is or becomes a part of the public domain without a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (c) The receiving party obtains from a third party under conditions permitting its disclosure to others; (d) Is independently developed by the receiving party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to judicial action or government regulations, provided that the receiving party notifies the furnishing party prior to such disclosure and co-operates with the furnishing party in the event the furnishing party elects to legally contest and avoid such disclosure. Notwithstanding the above, information originally disclosed under this agreement but later falling under (b), (c), or (d) shall remain confidential for one year from the date it met the new criteria.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a sentence stating that even if information falls under exceptions (b), (c), or (d), it remains confidential for one year. This directly contradicts the original exceptions, which previously allowed for immediate release of confidential restrictions. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case, and since the added text directly contradicts the exceptions previously provided, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The furnishing of Confidential Information pursu ant to this Agreement shall not be construed as granting or conferring, either expr essly or implicitly, any rights, licences or relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: The furnishing of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement may be construed as granting or conferring, either expressly or implicitly, certain rights, licenses, or relationships, depending on the specific context of the disclosure. The furnishing of Confidential Information pursu ant to this Agreement shall not be construed as granting or conferring, either expr essly or implicitly, any rights, licences or relationships.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of the conditional statement 'may be construed...' directly contradicts the original, absolute statement 'shall not be construed...'. This introduces uncertainty about whether the act of disclosing confidential information can create legal obligations or grant implied rights. The added phrase introduces ambiguity about whether the act of disclosing creates rights or not, this will lead to uncertainty as to each part's responsibility during a potential business undertanking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The furnishing of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement may be construed as granting or conferring, either expressly or implicitly, certain rights, licenses, or relationships, depending on the specific context of the disclosure. The furnishing of Confidential Information pursu ant to this Agreement shall not be construed as granting or conferring, either expr essly or implicitly, any rights, licences or relationships.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states the furnishing of confidential information 'shall not' be construed as granting rights. The changed text introduces the possibility that it 'may' be so construed, creating ambiguity and a direct conflict. The contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text and includes the contradictory statement, which causes the contradiction. Therefore, the changed text creates a contradiction within the document based on the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction: 'Structural flaws also cause in-text contradictions when a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.'

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter , including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights. All tangible information including, without limitat ion, documents, schematics, drawings, photographs, specifications, specimens or any other information submitted by either party to the other, will remain the property of the furnishing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter, including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing. All tangible information including, without limitation, documents, schematics, drawings, photographs, specifications, specimens or any other information submitted by either party to the other, will become joint property of both parties, after five (5) years of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing' introduces a contradiction to the initial statement that each party retains *all* rights. Also, it creates contradiction in ownership, since the original text say that the material submitted 'will remain the property of the furnishing party', the added text state 'will become joint property'. It makes it unclear how ownership is held. This could lead to disputes over intellectual property ownership. It is not clear which clause holds more value.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter, including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights, unless otherwise agreed upon in writing. All tangible information including, without limitation, documents, schematics, drawings, photographs, specifications, specimens or any other information submitted by either party to the other, will become joint property of both parties, after five (5) years of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding intellectual property ownership. The original text states each party retains 'all' rights, whereas the changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed upon in writing'. Furthermore, the original text asserts that tangible information 'will remain the property of the furnishing party,' while the changed text says it 'will become joint property of both parties, after five (5) years of the agreement.' This creates a direct conflict in ownership terms, making it unclear which clause holds precedence. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, highlighting the self-contradictory nature of the modification. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed with conflicting implications, creating uncertainty.
